commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Telegram transcript mirror guard that skips appending a delivery-mirror assistant row when the latest non-mirror assistant text matches, updates one Telegram dispatch test, and adds an unused transcript-tail helper/import.

PR surface: Source +67, Tests -7. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source reproduction, but not live-reproduced in this review. The linked issue supplies concrete JSONL evidence, and current main shows the direct Telegram mirror append plus cache-ttl/session leaf behavior matching the reported failure shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor after-fix real behavior proof and maintainer selection across sibling candidate fixes; automation cannot supply the contributor's real Telegram/WebChat evidence.

target.title: fix(telegram): skip mirror write when primary reply already exists (#94930)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94930.

Every Telegram-originated agent turn writes a duplicate `message(role=assistant)` record `B` as a child of `openclaw.cache-ttl` ~1s after the primary reply `A`. `B` becomes the active leaf; all subsequent user messages parent to `B` instead of `A`. `A` becomes an orphaned branch, causing:

- Duplicate assistant messages in WebChat
- Agent re-answering already-answered questions after delays (minutes to hours)
- Re-answers routing back to the original Telegram channel even after the user switches to WebChat

**Observed on v2026.6.8 — 100% reproducible from Telegram, not reproducible from WebChat native.**

## Root cause

Two writers touch the session transcript per Telegram turn:

- **A**: embedded runner (`appendAssistantMessageToSessionTranscript`)
- **B**: `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript()` in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — calls `appendSessionTranscriptMessage()` **directly**, bypassing the facade and its delivery-mirror dedup helpers

After prompt+compaction, `attempt.ts` appends an `openclaw.cache-ttl` custom node which becomes the active leaf. ~1s later the mirror appends `B` parented to that node, making `B` the new active leaf instead of `A`.

`appendSessionTranscriptMessageLocked` has an idempotency check via `readMessageIdempotencyKey`, but the mirror writer's constructed message has **no `idempotencyKey`** field — the check returns `undefined` and is skipped, so `B` is written unconditionally.

**Note:** commit `f5eddc2b6d` patched `findLatestEquivalentAssistantMessageId` in `transcript.ts` and the WebChat rendering layer, but the Telegram mirror writer calls `appendSessionTranscriptMessage` directly — that facade patch is on an entirely different call path and does not prevent the duplicate write.

## Real behavior evidence

Reproduced on a personal OpenClaw setup with `session 030bea8e`. Full JSONL evidence and tree diagram posted in the original issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94930#issuecomment-4752524069

Key excerpt from live JSONL:

```
14:25:09  message  id=95dbc41a  par=74d45537  role=assistant   ← A (primary reply, embedded runner)
14:25:09  custom   id=bfa1edcb  par=95dbc41a  kind=cache-ttl   ← cache-ttl becomes active leaf
14:25:10  message  id=8d1b4c19  par=bfa1edcb  role=assistant   ← B (delivery-mirror, parented to cache-ttl)
14:26:33  message  id=c3f2a801  par=8d1b4c19  role=user        ← next user msg parents to B, not A
```

Trigger: **Telegram → WebChat cross-channel only.** WebChat-native turns do not produce `B`.

## Fix

In `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript()` (`extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`), before calling `appendSessionTranscriptMessage`, read the transcript tail via `readLatestAssistantTextFromSessionTranscript` and compare text. If they match, the primary runner already wrote the reply — skip the mirror write entirely.

The helper naturally skips `delivery-mirror` and other gateway-injected entries, so it correctly identifies the embedded-runner primary reply.

Changes:
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — guard in `mirrorTelegramAssistantReplyToTranscript` (~15 lines net)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` — updated tests

## Testing

`pnpm test extensions/telegram` → **2162 passed, 0 failed**
